question.text: In a REIT IPO, strategic investors shall invest not less than ___% and not more than ___% of the total offer size.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, strategic investor(s) shall, either jointly or severally, invest not less than 5% and not more than 25% of the total offer size and enter into a binding unit subscription agreement with the Manager. **The Trap:** These percentages are identical to InvIT strategic investor requirements (5-25%), providing consistency across both frameworks. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Strategic investors in both REITs and InvITs: 5-25% of offer size, price not less than issue price, 180-day lock-in. The identical requirements simplify compliance.
